titleStmt.title: SII 24.9: original edition by H. K. Narasimhaswamy
No. 9. (A.R. No. 95 of 1936-37.) SLAB KEPT NEAR THE DĒVASTHĀNAM MUSEUM.

summary.p: Records a gift by Brahmādhirājaṉ alias Āchchiyaṉ-Bhaṭṭaṉ Śrī Vāsudēvaṉ Chakrapāṇi of Peruveṅgūr (vide No. 7 above) of two pieces of land in Śāsanamaṅgalam, a tax-free brahmadēya of his own in Viḷā-nāḍu, for sweet cake offerings to the god on the day of Ēkādaśi, during the Paṅguṉi festival in the temple. Mentions the measure called ‘Rājakēsari’. The record is dated in the 41st year (948-49 A.D.) of the king. cf. No. 7 above.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . matiraiyum Īḻamuṅ koṇṭa ko
pparakesarivanmakku yāṇṭu 4 10 1Āvatu
śrī Araṅkattu Emperumāṉukku viḷā nāṭṭu
brahmadeyam peruveṅkūr brahmādhirājaṉā
kiya Ācciyaṉ bhaṭṭaṉ śrī vāsudevadvakrapā
ṇi yeṉ paṅkuṉit tiruviḻā veḻuntaruḷi
ṉa Aiñcā nāḷ tiru vekādaśi peṟṟa pūśatti ṉā
ḷ Appap perun tiruvamutu ceytaruḷa Eṉ
7 10 1 bhoga brahmadeyamāṉa Iṟaiyili viḷā
nāṭṭu śāsaṉamaṅkalattuk kuṭutta nilattuk ki
caitta Ellai peruveṅkūr pilāṟṟukku
meṟkum naṭuvil vāykkālukku vaṭakkum
nāṉey muṉ tiruvorośaṇit tiruvārā
dhaṉaikkuk kuṭutta nilattukkuk kiḻakkum peru
veṅkūr pilāṟṟukkey teṟku mivicaitta
peru nāṉ kellaiyi lakappaṭṭa nilam Eṭṭu mā
vum Iṉṉa mitaṉukkey kuṭutta nilattuk ke
llai nāṉey tiruvorośaṇit tiruvārā
dhaṉaikkuk kuṭutta nilattukku meṟkum naṭuve
yoṭiṉa vāykkālukkey vaṭakkum nāṟṟuk
kāl Araikkāṇi yiraṇṭukku marai mā viraṇṭuk
kum kiḻakkum peruveṅkūrp pilāṟṟukkut
tekku māka Iñāṉ kellaiyilu makappa
ṭṭa nilam kāleyarai māvumāka Iraṇṭu ki
ṭakkaiyumāy ñilamaraiyey mum māva
raiyāl rājakesariyā laḷakku nel nūṟṟu
Eṇ kala micaitta nikki nel nūṟṟoruka
laṉe Iru tūṇiyāṟ cellum paṭi Appap
perun tiruvamutiṉukkup pat teṭṭuk kut
tal paḻavariśi yirukalam ney tūṇip patakku
Āṉpāl kalam parupputtūṇi carkkarai ppana
vāḻaip paḻa meḻunūṟu teṅkam pa
miḷaku śīraka muṉṉāḻi tirupparaṅkuṉ
kkup poḷiru ka�Incomplete.
Digital edition of SII 24.9 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
